title: wikstroemia trichotoma var. trichotoma
intro: Wikstroemia trichotoma var. trichotoma, also known as the Chinese rosemary, is a plant species belonging to the family Thymelaeaceae, native to China and Japan. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting digestion, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. In herbal preparations, Wikstroemia trichotoma var. trichotoma is often used as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves are also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments.
